issue_title: Couple of links broken and invalid redirects in Keycloak Docs, probably due to a change to Docker docs
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The Keycloak Documentation CI caught few issues with broken links and invalid redirects from here - https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/6581652421/job/17881947174#step:5:1007

```
[INFO] Results:
[INFO] 
[ERROR] Failures: 
[ERROR]   ExternalLinksTest.checkExternalLinks:41 Broken links (1) in guide 'securing_apps': 
                - https://docs.docker.com/registry/configuration/ (invalid status code 404)
[ERROR]   ExternalLinksTest.checkExternalLinks:41 Broken links (2) in guide 'server_admin': 
                - https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/auth/ (invalid redirect to /registry/)
                - https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/auth/token/ (invalid redirect to /registry/)
[INFO] 
[ERROR] Tests run: 6, Failures: 2, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

```

An update from the Docker about the Registry being donated to CNCF and moved to a different location can be found here.

https://docs.docker.com/registry/ 

```
Registry
Registry, the open source implementation for storing and distributing container images and other content, has been donated to the CNCF. Registry now goes under the name of Distribution, and the documentation has moved to distribution.github.io/distribution.

For reference documentation on the API protocol that Distribution implements, see Registry HTTP API.

For documentation related to authentication, see:

Token authentication specification
OAuth 2.0 token authentication
JWT authentication
Token scope and access
For information about image manifests, see:

Image Manifest Version 2, Schema 2
Image Manifest Version 2, Schema 1 (deprecated)
```


### Version

nightly (10/20/2023)

### Expected behavior

The links are expected to take the user to a valid landing page

### Actual behavior

The links are either broken or having invalid redirects due to a change in the Docker Registry docs location change.

### How to Reproduce?

Run the below command locally

```
MVN_HTTP_CONFIG="-Dhttp.keepAlive=false -Dmaven.wagon.http.pool=false -Dmaven.wagon.http.retryHandler.class=standard -Dmaven.wagon.http.retryHandler.count=3 -Dmaven.wagon.httpconnectionManager.ttlSeconds=120"

mvn install -Dtest=ExternalLinksTest -am -pl docs/documentation/tests -nsu -B -e -Pdocumentation $MVN_HTTP_CONFIG
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Cannot disable LDAP-backed user if importEnabled=false
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

Exception looks like this:
```
[org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.UserResource] (executor-thread-9) Could not update user!: org.keycloak.models.ModelException: Not possible to write 'enabled' when updating user 'user1'
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.ReadOnlyUserModelDelegate.readOnlyException(ReadOnlyUserModelDelegate.java:146)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.ReadOnlyUserModelDelegate.setEnabled(ReadOnlyUserModelDelegate.java:51)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setEnabled(UserModelDelegate.java:65)
	at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.LDAPWritesOnlyUserModelDelegate.setEnabled(LDAPWritesOnlyUserModelDelegate.java:58)
...
        at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.mappers.msad.MSADUserAccountControlStorageMapper$MSADUserModelDelegate.setEnabled(MSADUserAccountControlStorageMapper.java:253)
...
        at org.keycloak.storage.adapter.UpdateOnlyChangeUserModelDelegate.setEnabled(UpdateOnlyChangeUserModelDelegate.java:55)
```

### Version

22.0.4

### Expected behavior

Disabling a user works and propagates bits to LDAP via MSAD user account controls

### Actual behavior

Disabling a user fails with a 400 error complaining that the UserModel `enabled` attribute can't be written. Seems like one of these decorators (LDAPWritesOnlyUserModelDelegate?) is unaware of what MSADUserAccountControlStorageMapper is doing?

### How to Reproduce?

Create WRITEABLE LDAP federated storage, disable import. Create a user and set a password.

THEN Attempt to disable a user with the admin UI (or REST API via PUT request).


### Anything else?

The bug is a little tricky because it only happens on user modifications and not initial user creation: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/7b97272b23a9cd42596d05e8a4f4dd05d4bfa0e1/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPStorageProvider.java#L218
commit_message:
Align isEnabled in MSAD mappers to how other properties are processed in UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper

- user model is updated by onImport with the enabled/disabled status of the LDAP user
- a config option always.read.enabled.value.from.ldap was introduced, in synch to what we have in UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper
- isEnabled checks the flag to decide if it should always retrieve the value from LDAP, or return the local value.
- setEnabled first updates the LDAP tx, and then calls the delegate to avoid issue #24201

Closes #26695
Closed #24201

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: admin-fine-grained-authz + client authorization settings requires view-client role
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When using the preview feature admin-fine-grained-authz to allow certain users to manage their clients, some clients are using the Authorization capabilities. In Keycloak <=v19 using the old Admin UI Console, the only realm-management client roles that were needed to these users were: manage-authorization, query-clients, query-users, query-groups. In the new Admin UI Console, the role view-clients is required to create resources, client policy, or authorization scope in the Authorization settings for the client.

This is a problem as view-clients role allows users to view other clients and the client secrets of all applications.

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

When using admin-fine-grained-authz settings users with manage permissions to a client should be able to configure and create authorization policies for their client without requiring access to ALL clients in the realm.

### Actual behavior

To create new Authorization policies, client resources, or authorization scopes the user must have `view-client` realm management client role.

### How to Reproduce?

- Follow instructions at [Fine Grain Admin Permissions](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_fine_grain_permissions) to configure a user to manage one specific client.
- Add realm-management client role of manage-authorization to the user as well.
- Turn on Authorization for the client.
- Login as user to the security-admin-console for the realm.
- View the configured managed client and select the Authorization tab.
- Select one of these sub-tabs of Authorization - Resources, Scopes, Policies
- On the tab, click the `Create (X)` button like `Create resource` button on the Resources sub-tab.
- You'll be met with an error message: Forbidden, permission needed: view-clients

### Anything else?

Related to: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/23294
commit_message:
Allow authorization changes with fine-grained client access and manage-authorization. (#25280)

Signed-off-by: Stan Silvert <ssilvert@redhat.com>

issue_title: CVE-2023-44483 - Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File vulnerability in org.apache.santuario:xmlsec
issue_body:
### Description

- Package Manager: maven
- Vulnerable module: org.apache.santuario:xmlsec
- Introduced through: org.keycloak:[keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT), org.keycloak:[keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT) and others

### Detailed paths

- _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:[keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT) › org.keycloak:[keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT) › org.apache.santuario:xmlsec@2.2.3

# Overview

[org.apache.santuario:xmlsec](https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.apache.santuario/xmlsec) is a package to provide implementation of the primary security standards for XML, XML-Signature Syntax and Processing and XML Encryption Syntax and Processing.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File when using the JSR 105 API. An attacker can disclose a private key in log files by generating an XML Signature and enabling logging with debug level.

# Remediation

Upgrade `org.apache.santuario:xmlsec` to version 2.2.6, 2.3.4, 3.0.3 or higher.

# References

- [Apache Advisory](https://lists.apache.org/thread/vmqbp9mfxtrf0kmbnnmbn3h9j6dr9q55)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/apache/santuario-xml-security-java/commit/18999b9dced2c736f4a8d52d0c7d1b114351c77d)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/apache/santuario-xml-security-java/commit/c85db6be7f49815253f59902b066086a7ad5ce9a)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/apache/santuario-xml-security-java/commit/cd923d63ba2a02578b263258e749f3ed94389fd8)
commit_message:
CVE-2023-44483 - Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File vulnerability in org.apache.santuario:xmlsec

Closes #24224

### Area

### Describe the bug

The following error is generated if an LDAP provider User federation is offline.  This error shows up when trying to list all users.

![Keycloak LDAP offline Error](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/59295730/c9c99815-ad9b-4dd8-ae29-0892b1683e7d)


### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

Local users and users from other LDAP providers should be listed.  Users from the offline Federation should be listed but can be tagged as offline.

### Actual behavior

The error in the screenshot above is displayed.  Clicking the "Press here to refersh and continue" takes you  back tot he master realm roles tab

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure a working LDAP provider in User Federation. I used both Active Directory and OpenLDAP.  These work perfectly once keycloak can connect to the these LDAP services.
2. Take one or both LDAP service offline
3. Navigate to Users tab and Search for all user by supplying "*"

### Anything else?

My work-around is to disable the LDAP provider that is offline.
Once that is done all users both local and from other online providers are returned when searching.

issue_title: User attribute array data gets stored as comma-separated value if no specific input type annotation present
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When a user attribute value contains multiple values and no annotation is set to render it as multi-value field, it is rendered as text field with comma-separated string. Editing this string causes the comma separated string value to be stored.
The original array of values is replaced with a single value.

![screen-record](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/789496/8f593ffb-a26b-498d-bb46-5e3b1be834ca)



### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

The attribute with multipe-values should not get lost.

### Actual behavior

A comma separated string is stored.

### How to Reproduce?

1) Store multiple values in an attribute of a user, e.g. by 

 - configuring input type 'multiselect'
 - update a user and add two values
 - remove input type annotation

2) Update the user. The input is rendered as simple text field.

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/789496/5df5e068-8ddb-4c3f-a294-6d6c77340a21)

3) Add an entry as if you would believe it should be done (",newentry") and save the user



### Anything else?

This bug may cause accidental data corruption by Admins.

Thought that this bug was already reported and fixed in #20837. 
Maybe it was re-introduced with #23911?

issue_title: CVE-2023-4586 - Hot Rod client does not enable hostname validation when using TLS that lead to a MITM attack
issue_body:
### Description

A vulnerability was found in the Hot Rod client. This security issue occurs as the Hot Rod client does not enable hostname validation when using TLS, possibly resulting in a man-in-the-middle (MITM) attack.

Additional details:

The issue is impacted by a vulnerability into the Netty transitive dependency.

[io.netty:netty-handler](https://github.com/netty/netty.git/netty-handler) is a library that provides an asynchronous event-driven network application framework and tools for rapid development of maintainable high performance and high scalability protocol servers and clients. In other words, Netty is a NIO client server framework which enables quick and easy development of network applications such as protocol servers and clients. It greatly simplifies and streamlines network programming such as TCP and UDP socket server.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Certificate Validation. Certificate hostname validation is disabled by default in `Netty 4.1.x` which makes it potentially susceptible to Man-in-the-Middle attacks.

Source:
- https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/CVE-2023-4586
- https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-IONETTY-1042268
commit_message:
CVE-2023-4586 - Hot Rod client does not enable hostname validation when using TLS that lead to a MITM attack

A vulnerability was found in the Hot Rod client. This security issue
occurs as the Hot Rod client does not enable hostname validation when
using TLS, possibly resulting in a man-in-the-middle (MITM) attack.

Closes #24328

Signed-off-by: Bruno Oliveira da Silva <bruno@abstractj.com>

issue_title: Keycloak operator tries to manipulate Secret which is not managed by Keycloak
issue_body:

### Area

### Describe the bug

In the Keycloak operator v22.0.5 logs errors are reported about the Secret which is not managed neither referenced in the Keycloak CR.

Operator reports attempts to manipulate Secret `keycloak-bindinfo-cs-keycloak-tls-secret`, even though such Secret is not referenced in the Keycloak CR. However, subject Secret indeed exists in the same namespace, but it's owned by other operator running in the same namespace.

issue_title: Adding scopes via registration_client_uri does not work when using Dynamic Client Registration
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

I'm using Dynamic Client Registration to register new Clients/Service Accounts to access back-end APIs. In my use-cases, I use scopes, that map to different API-Products to grant access to these APIs. This means that I need to be able to add scopes to an existing Client to give them access to new APIs.

When I register a new client using DCR, I can specify the scopes I want this client to have in the request:
```
{
   "application_type": "native",
   "client_name": "My Cool Client",
   "logo_uri": "https://client.example.com/logo.png",
   "contacts": ["duncan.doyle@example.com"],
   "grant_types": ["client_credentials"],
   "scope": "Catstronauts"
}
```
Sending this request to the OIDC registration endpoint correctly creates my Client with the "Catstronauts" scope as its single scope.

When I later try to add the "Petstore" scope using the "registration_client_uri" and "registration_access_token", that were returned in the response when I registered the client, using the following PUT request, the "Petstore" scope does not get added:
```
{
   "client_id": {generated-id-when-I-registered-my-client},
   "client_name": "My new client name",
   "scope": "Catstronauts Petstore"
}
```

However, the `client_name` does get changed, which shows that the request is getting processed.

Also, I get a `200` as the response code and there are no errors in the Keycloak logs.

Note that in the "Client Registration -> Authenticated access policies -> Allowed Client Scopes", I've listed all the scopes I want to configure.

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

When I add a scope to a client via a `PUT` operation to its `registration_client_uri`, the scope should be added to the client.

### Actual behavior

The scope does not get added to the client.

### How to Reproduce?

Register a new client via the OIDC Registration Endpoint (e.g. http://{keycloak}/realms/master/clients-registrations/openid-connect), for example by sending the following `POST` request:
```
{
   "application_type": "native",
   "client_name": "My Cool Client",
   "logo_uri": "https://client.example.com/logo.png",
   "contacts": ["duncan.doyle@example.com"],
   "grant_types": ["client_credentials"],
   "scope": "Catstronauts"
}
```

From the response, grab the `registation_client_uri` and `registration_access_token`. Send a `PUT` request to the `registration_client_uri`, trying to add a scope:
```
{
   "client_id": {generated-id-when-I-registered-my-client},
   "client_name": "My new client name",
   "scope": "Catstronauts Petstore"
}
```

Scope does not get added.

### Anything else?

I know that there are other Keycloak REST APIs I can use to add a scope to a Client, but I would like to use the DCR standard, as my platform needs to support multiple IdPs, and hence I would like to rely on this standard as much as possible.

NOTE: The parameter `scope` is not in the OIDC client registration specification, but it is in OAuth 2 client registration specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7591#section-2

Update client scopes in Client Update Request in DCR

Fix ClientScopesClientRegistrationPolicy.beforeUpdate because it was modifying the original clientRepresentation.
Add updateClientScopes method to set client scopes in Client Update Request in DCR.

Closes #24361

issue_title: UpdateUserLocaleAction does not trigger EventType.UPDATE_PROFILE event
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

When "Update User Locale" required action is active, it does not fire "EventType.UPDATE_PROFILE" event, although user profile is updated.

_No response_

issue_title: cache-stack option should be lowercase
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

> ```
> bin/kc.[sh|bat] build --cache-stack=<stack>
> ```
> The default stack is set to `UDP` when distributed caches are enabled.

In this sentence, UDP should be lowercase udp.

```bash
$ bin/kc.sh build --cache-stack=UDP
Invalid value for option '--cache-stack': UDP. Expected values are: tcp, udp, kubernetes, ec2, azure, google
Try 'kc.sh build --help' for more information on the available options.
```



### Version

21.1.1

```bash
$ bin/kc.sh build --cache-stack=UDP
Invalid value for option '--cache-stack': UDP. Expected values are: tcp, udp, kubernetes, ec2, azure, google
Try 'kc.sh build --help' for more information on the available options.
```

### Actual behavior

```bash
$ bin/kc.sh build --cache-stack=udp
Updating the configuration and installing your custom providers, if any. Please wait.
2023-10-30 09:27:09,440 INFO  [io.quarkus.deployment.QuarkusAugmentor] (main) Quarkus augmentation completed in 12338ms
Server configuration updated and persisted. Run the following command to review the configuration:

	kc.sh show-config
```

### How to Reproduce?

```bash
$ bin/kc.sh build --cache-stack=UDP
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Query on update the ADFS FederationMetadata.xml on the keycloak instead of delete and recreating the IDP config #24310
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

when we use identity provider( example: ADFS, Ping Identity, Okta,Azure etc ) for SSO, If the IDP certificate has expired and has been replaced , Does the FederationMetadata.xml on the keycloak for the IDP also need to be replaced ?

If yes how can we update the ADFS FederationMetadata.xml on the keycloak?

Keycloak version: 22.0.4

### Version

22.0.4

### Expected behavior

no need to replace FederationMetadata.xml on the keycloak for the IDP

### Actual behavior

Delete the IDP client , recreate  IDP client and  replace FederationMetadata.xml on the keycloak for the IDP

### How to Reproduce?

when we use identity provider( example: ADFS, Ping Identity, Okta,Azure etc ) for SSO, If the IDP certificate has expired and has been replaced, we need to delete the IDP client, recreate IDP client and replace FederationMetadata.xml on the keycloak for the IDP

This is not a best practice on production system.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Users overview: No more hint is shown that you need to search in order to see users with user federation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When enabling user federation in a realm users are not fetched automatically when going to the Users overview. Previously (I think for KC versions < 22 - cant confirm that) a different Information was shown, indicating that you have to enter a '*' or something to search in order to fetch users.

With KC 22.0.5 at least no such hint is displayed, indicating that you must search and that this is due to user federation:  
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/1793007/4a5c017b-e2b9-4542-a071-77e9f934cbd6)

This might be confusing as one might assume there are no users in the realm even though there are.

**The functionality is not affected!**

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

Indicate clearly that you have to provide a search value because of User Federation.

### Actual behavior

It might not be clear enough on to why no users are displayed and/or what the user has to do. There is also no more indication that a * could be used in the search.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a realm
2. Add user federation

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Admin UI: User Profile Attribute Group Displayname not translated in Create/Update User Screens
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The displayName of a User Profile Attribute group is not being translated on the create or update user screens: 

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/1793007/ca3aedf8-22b8-4a9e-b842-d19dfe246359)

It seems like the group display name is not passed through the translate function.

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

The displayName of a User Profile Attribute Group should be translated like the displayName of the attributes themselves.

### Actual behavior

The displayName of a User Profile Attribute Group is not translated.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a realm
2. Enable user profile
3. Add a user profile attribute group and give it a translateable displayname, e.g. "${personalInfo}"
4. Add a user profile attribute and assign it to the group
5. Go to user create/details screen, seeing that the ${...} around the label is not removed indicates that is not running through the translate function




### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Email-verified checkbox not visible anymore when user profile is enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When enabling the user profile on the General-tab in Realm settings, the user edit-page doesn't show a "email verified" toggle button anymore. Over [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/js/apps/admin-ui/src/user/UserForm.tsx#L189) I can see that another component is being rendered when the user profile is enabled, but [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/js/apps/admin-ui/src/user/UserProfileFields.tsx) it's not visible. Maybe because this field should be provided by the database, but I am not sure.

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

An "email-verified"-checkbox should be available regardless of the "user profile enabled"-switch.

### Actual behavior

The "email-verified"-checkbox is not available

### How to Reproduce?

- Enable the feature `account3`, 
- Enable the "user profile" with the switch in Realm settings,
- Go to a users details tab. 

The checkbox won't be here.

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Area

### Describe the bug

Hi.

I'm working with KeyCloak (_both, with a Docker image and a locally downloaded v22.0.5_) and a custom mapper for OIDC Tokens.

Basically, I've used the code in this URL:
https://betterjavacode.com/programming/custom-protocol-mapper-keycloak

I've generated a JAR and put it into the /opt/keycloak/providers folder. Let's call it: "`provider-cpm_1.0.0.jar`".

Later, I've used it (I can see it as a mapper) in a custom scope within a own Realm's client. It works.
( KeyCloak in a docker container from: keycloak/keycloak:latest )

### How to Reproduce?

issue_title: Remove non-unique `id` attributes from `webauthn-authenticate.ftl`
issue_body:
The `webauthn-authenticate.ftl` template has several `id` attributes that are inside of a loop, these are therefore likely not unique, which is a violation of the HTML standard. The following `id` attributes will have to be made globally unique, or more likely converted to `class` attributes:

- [`kc-webauthn-authenticator`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e96d6b38a8710366b7b38f8d3a895854f9e4f946/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/webauthn-authenticate.ftl#L33)
- [`kc-webauthn-authenticator-label`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e96d6b38a8710366b7b38f8d3a895854f9e4f946/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/webauthn-authenticate.ftl#L38C50-L38C81)
- [`kc-webauthn-authenticator-transport`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e96d6b38a8710366b7b38f8d3a895854f9e4f946/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/webauthn-authenticate.ftl#L44)
- [`kc-webauthn-authenticator-created-label`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e96d6b38a8710366b7b38f8d3a895854f9e4f946/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/webauthn-authenticate.ftl#L56)
- [`kc-webauthn-authenticator-created`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e96d6b38a8710366b7b38f8d3a895854f9e4f946/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/webauthn-authenticate.ftl#L59)

### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When there is a key component without `privateKey` available in the database, there might be NullPointerException thrown when listing keys of the particular realm.

I was able to reproduce this just by manually removing some `privateKey` from the DB. So steps like:
- Create realm
- Run manually some SQL command similar to this in your DB:
```
delete from COMPONENT_CONFIG where NAME='privateKey' and COMPONENT_ID='59b6ecde-03a0-42e7-9b0f-39b5bda3b2e0'; # Replace with proper component ID, which refers to some key in your realm
```
- Login to admin console, go to your realm and list keys. There is exception like this in server.log:
```
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "java.security.Key.getEncoded()" because "key" is null
	at org.keycloak.common.util.KeyUtils.createKeyId(KeyUtils.java:78)
	at org.keycloak.keys.AbstractRsaKeyProvider.createKeyWrapper(AbstractRsaKeyProvider.java:78)
	at org.keycloak.keys.AbstractRsaKeyProvider.createKeyWrapper(AbstractRsaKeyProvider.java:68)
	at org.keycloak.keys.ImportedRsaKeyProvider.loadKey(ImportedRsaKeyProvider.java:54)
	at org.keycloak.keys.AbstractRsaKeyProvider.<init>(AbstractRsaKeyProvider.java:55)
	at org.keycloak.keys.ImportedRsaKeyProvider.<init>(ImportedRsaKeyProvider.java:38)
	at org.keycloak.keys.GeneratedRsaKeyProviderFactory.create(GeneratedRsaKeyProviderFactory.java:46)
	at org.keycloak.keys.DefaultKeyManager.lambda$getProviders$10(DefaultKeyManager.java:259)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$3$1.accept(ReferencePipeline.java:197)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.SortedOps$SizedRefSortingSink.end(SortedOps.java:357)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:510)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.wrapAndCopyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:499)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReduceOps$ReduceOp.evaluateSequential(ReduceOps.java:921)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.evaluate(AbstractPipeline.java:234)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.collect(ReferencePipeline.java:682)
	at org.keycloak.keys.DefaultKeyManager.getProviders(DefaultKeyManager.java:268)
	at org.keycloak.keys.DefaultKeyManager.getKeysStream(DefaultKeyManager.java:149)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.KeyResource.getKeyMetadata(KeyResource.java:69)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:77)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:568)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.core.MethodInjectorImpl.invoke(MethodInjectorImpl.java:154)
```

This issue is just about improve error handling a bit, so instead of NullPointerException, we have some more meaningful exception message in the server.log (possibly displaying the affected realm, the affected key provider from the realm with some info about it etc). Also with instructions that admin should probably manually fix or delete the particular key in the particular realm.

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

Better exception message in server.log

### Actual behavior

NullPointerException in server.log

### How to Reproduce?

See above. It is corner case, but it is proved to happened for some deployments...

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Broken (read-only) database connections not getting removed from connection pool, keycloak claims to be healthy.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

We have a master/standby setup of our postgres database (standard zalando/postgres-operator).

We had a "hiccup" in our k8s cluster, which resulted in  a failover - master and standby got switched. The peculiar thing about this switch was that the former master survived the hiccup, only the former standby got restarted and promoted to master.

This resulted in all connections to the database in the Keycloak pool still being active, but now pointing to the standby database, meaning they are read-only.

From that moment on, Keycloak stopped working properly. Every login failed with this exception:

```
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.internal.ResultSetReturnImpl.executeUpdate(ResultSetReturnImpl.java:197)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at io.agroal.pool.wrapper.PreparedStatementWrapper.executeUpdate(PreparedStatementWrapper.java:88)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at com.sun.proxy.$Proxy86.executeUpdate(Unknown Source)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at org.postgresql.ds.PGPooledConnection$StatementHandler.invoke(PGPooledConnection.java:441)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:566)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at jdk.internal.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor181.invoke(Unknown Source)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at org.postgresql.jdbc.PgPreparedStatement.executeUpdate(PgPreparedStatement.java:152)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at org.postgresql.jdbc.PgPreparedStatement.executeWithFlags(PgPreparedStatement.java:190)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at org.postgresql.jdbc.PgStatement.execute(PgStatement.java:413)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at org.postgresql.jdbc.PgStatement.executeInternal(PgStatement.java:496)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at org.postgresql.core.v3.QueryExecutorImpl.execute(QueryExecutorImpl.java:356)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at org.postgresql.core.v3.QueryExecutorImpl.processResults(QueryExecutorImpl.java:2366)
2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
	at org.postgresql.core.v3.QueryExecutorImpl.receiveErrorResponse(QueryExecutorImpl.java:2676)

2023-10-31 18:47:47.096	
Caused by: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: cannot execute INSERT in a read-only transaction
```

This could only be resolved by manually restarting the keycloak server to force a reconnection to the new master database.


### Version

quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:20.0.5 installed via helm chart

### Expected behavior

Database connection that cause exceptions should be removed from the pool and not be reused. No manual intervention should be necessary.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak is using the non-functional database connections ad inifinitum and doesn't care about exceptions. Health check is reporting "healthy", though the database connection is read only.

### How to Reproduce?

If you do not want to setup the whole HA feature, start Keycloak with a local Postgres and set it to read only: 
`alter system set default_transaction_read_only=on;`

### Anything else?

We have a lot of Spring application in our cluster. They are using the Hikari connection pool. This pool can be configured so that database connections which run into an exception are always and immediately removed from the connection pool. This eventually auto-heals the application, as the read only connection get dropped and replaced by fresh connections to the master database.

As Keycloak is using the Agroal pool, I found this is the documenation:

> exceptionSorter(ExceptionSorter) - This extension point tells Agroal which exceptions indicate that the connection is in an invalid state and should be removed from the pool. Agroal provides a few implementations of the ExceptionSorter interface for selected databases. By default, no exception is considered fatal.

It would be nice if you could activate this feature.

Connect to primary PostgreSQL instance by default (#36330)

issue_title: Deadlock when pre-loading remote sessions from external Infinispan
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Keycloak preloads remote sessions from a remote Infinispan. 

When analyzing concurrency tests, where some nodes arrive and leave, this can lead to a `putAll()` operation to deadlock as seen in the test log. 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/833bf9864356abe6f2c9f672edf1438b8635f48c/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/remotestore/RemoteCacheSessionsLoader.java#L144

Given the latest setup configurations in the KCB project, instead of preloading the entries Keycloak could rely on the Infinispan fetching the entries from the remote store automatically when needed, saving startup time. 



### Version

main

### Expected behavior

Use the remote store as persistence store as described in the KCB project.

### Actual behavior

With nodes coming and going, `putAll` might touch the same keys, and then deadlock.

### How to Reproduce?

issue_title: Keycloak operator 22.0.z created RealmImport Job withouth checking Keycloak operand health first
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

Using Keycloak 22.0.5 build, when the `KeycloakRealmImport` CR is created soon after the primary `Keycloak` CR (e.g. in the GitOps pipeline), the real import Job `<realm-import-cr-name>` is started unconditionally and the first few Pods created (up to 6 backoff limit) might be in the failed state, till Keycloak operand fully is initialized.

This ends up with some bad initial experience where the failed Pods are found on the cluster.

While I do understand that perhaps the best practice is to wait for the primary `Keycloak` CR would be reporting Ready in `.status`, before any other Keycloak CRs are created, but it's not something typically possible / used in the GitOps pipelines.

The proposed solution is to have Keycloak Operator checking the referenced Keycloak CR status, before submtting the real import Job.


```yaml
apiVersion: k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1
kind: KeycloakRealmImport
metadata:
  name: cs-cloudpak-realm
spec:
  keycloakCRName: cs-keycloak-test1 <--- here, the Keycloak CR cs-keycloak-test1 is created, but its status is not Ready
  realm:
    displayName: cloudpak
    enabled: true
    id: cloudpak
    realm: cloudpak
```

So I guess there is a basic check for existence of operand `StatefulSet` at https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/operator/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/controllers/KeycloakRealmImportController.java#L116C23-L116C23 , to check whether operand StatefulSet exists, but it's not checked whether it's running, before the import Job is submitted.

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

No failed Pods when KeycloakRealmImport CR is created immediatelly after Keycloak CR (before Keycloak is ready)

### Actual behavior

Failed Pods when KeycloakRealmImport CR is created immediatelly after Keycloak CR (before Keycloak is ready)

### How to Reproduce?

Create KeycloakRealmImport CR immediatelly after Keycloak CR

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
fix: check operand StatefulSet is ready before creating job (#24720)

closes #24526

Signed-off-by: Steve Hawkins <shawkins@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Piotr Godowski <Piotr.Godowski@pl.ibm.com>

issue_title: DPoP advertised on OIDC Well Known Endpoint even though DPoP feature is not enabled (preview feature)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

DPoP is advertised on the OIDC Well Known Endpoint even though the DPoP feature is not enabled - it is currently a preview feature.

In [RFC 9449](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9449#section-5.1) 'dpop_signing_alg_values_supported' in the OIDC Well Known Endpoint can be used to signal support for DPoP. 

Having this advertised as supported when the DPoP feature is not actually enabled may be misleading to clients that bootstrap their configuration automatically from this endpoint.

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

When DPoP feature is not enabled, `dpop_signing_alg_values_supported` in the OIDC Well Known Endpoint is either an empty array or missing entirely.

### Actual behavior

Regardless of DPoP feature status, `dpop_signing_alg_values_supported` in the OIDC Well Known Endpoint has all the supported algorithms mentioned.

### How to Reproduce?

* Start Keycloak without DPoP feature enabled
  * using podman `podman run -it --rm -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:nightly start-dev`
* Browse to http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration
* See `dpop_signing_alg_values_supported` set in metadata

### Anything else?

This is likely a minor issue. This also looks like it may be relatively simple to resolve and I will submit a PR.

Feature flag DPoP metadata in OIDC Well Known endpoint

issue_title: iframe for frontend logout gets blocked if a custom CSP header is used
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area core ### Describe the bug When Front channel logout is configured for a oidc client the logout is performed using an `iframe` to call the client front-channel URL. For example in my sample client: ```html
  • sample-rhsso
    • ``` The CSP header by default is modified to allow client URL in the `iframe` adding the hostname of the client backchannel URL to the `frame-src` poclicy. In my sample: ``` Content-Security-Policy: frame-src localhost:8081 ; object-src 'none'; ``` If a custom CSP header is configured in the realm the `frame-src` is not modified and therefore the iframe is not called because it's not allowed. For example is I change my realm `Realm Settings -> Security Defenses -> Content-Security-Policy` to `frame-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; object-src 'none'; style-src 'self';`. I'm just adding the `style-src` directive to the default CSP value but now the CSP received by the browser is: ``` Content-Security-Policy: frame-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; object-src 'none'; style-src 'self'; ``` And the backchannel URL is not called because it's not allowed by the CSP. So the client is not logged out. ### Version 22.0.5 ### Expected behavior I think we need a more intelligent design of the CSP header. I would do the following: * Manage `frame-src` and `frame-ancestors` independently of the other directives. * For `frame-src` add the hostnames needed to the current value defined by the realm (by default would be `'self'` and transformed to `'self` hostname1 hostname2...`. * For `frame-ancestors` remove the directive if and only if the default `'self'` is in place. This modification is needed for the login iframe used by the JS adapter. If the administrator has modified the `frame-ancestors` it will be respected and not modified. ### Actual behavior If the CSP header is customized at realm level the modifications for `frame-src` and `frame-ancestors` are just not done. The logout or the iframe for login won't work. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Create an OIDC client with Front channel logout to ON and Front-channel logout URL configured. 2. Test that by default the CSP and iframe is OK and the frontchannel URL is called. 3. Modify the realm setting CSP to `frame-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; object-src 'none'; style-src 'self';`. 4. Check the CSP is just sent as configured in the realm and the iframe URL is not called. ### Anything else? _No response_
commit_message:
Better management of the CSP header
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/24568

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Read Only Access of a realm clients' Authz is broken for Admin Console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Users in the master realm who have view-authorization role of a sub realm can't see the "Authorization" tab of the clients (that has authorization enabled) of that sub realm.

### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

Users in the master realm who have view-authorization role of a sub realm should be able to see the "Authorization" tab of the clients (that have authorization enabled) of that sub realm.

### Actual behavior

Users in the master realm who have view-authorization role of a sub realm can't see the "Authorization" tab of the clients (that has authorization enabled) of that sub realm.

### How to Reproduce?

1) Create a User in the master realm and assign these roles to that user (Make sure to assign your sub realm view-authorization role to that user).

![KC1](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/32916450/72672995-6a3f-4017-97a3-8d913f7ceb04)

2) Login/impersonate that user, then go to your sub realm clients and click on one of the clients in that realm that has Authorization enabled. 
You won't be able to see the "Authorization" tab even tho that you have the role view-authorization of that sub realm:

![KC2](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/32916450/341512bc-ed9f-46b6-89f9-ba5a96dfd248)



### Anything else?

If I assign the sub realm manage-authorization role to that user, the "Authorization" tab shows up, but of course, the user will have write access to the Authorizations tab, which is not a desirable solution for read-only users.

![KC3](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/32916450/70efd418-50ad-4dd5-a129-75ff196d77b1)

issue_title: Leaving Single Sign Out page open for too long and then confirming logout leads to error page
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

We have a logout page that is responsible for logging the user out of all connected services.
If a user goes to this logout page but leaves that page open for about an hour and only then presses the logout button to confirm the logout, the user is redirected to the error page from which there is no knowing what went wrong or how to recover (except navigating manually to the login page again via URL).
In the attached image the two steps are shown: Above is the logout page with the logout button and below the resulting error is shown.
![keycloak_error_logout](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/34295773/13b63968-ab20-42a3-87c0-00148610e915)


### Version

19.0.3, 22.0.4

### Expected behavior

As I suspect this issue to be related to some timeout setting, the expected behaviour should probably be to automatically redirect the user to the login page instead of an error page when the session expires while on the logout screen.

### Actual behavior

The user is redirected to an error page upon confirming the logout with the logout button on the logout page.

### How to Reproduce?

I am not sure if the problem is easily reproducible without our setup, but here are the steps I took to consistently reproduce this error on our system:

- From our application, go to the logout page for a logged in user.
- Leave this page open for about an hour.
- Confirm the logout by pressing the logout button.

### Anything else?

When filtering for logout errors in the keycloak admin console UI, the logout error event that is caused by this issue is shown but without any client or user, which probably indicates the session expired and; therefore, the event can not be matched to a logged in user.
![keycloak_error_log](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/34295773/65cac268-6a99-4dd7-b52c-ddb5ed26e99b)

Upgrading from keycloak 19.0.3 to 22.0.4 did not solve this issue for us.
commit_message:
Skip link only when client is not system when logout (#24595)

issue_title: Locale names are translated within Admin UI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The locale names are translated within the admin ui. This makes it very hard to configure supported languages.

See:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/314690/9e099c7d-8abf-4c2f-98cf-87d8c56a463d)


### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

The locale names should be listed in the locale of the current user or default locale.

### Actual behavior

The locales are translated into their respective native names.

### How to Reproduce?

1) Start a Keycloak 22.0.5 
2) Enable localization
3) configure languages


### Anything else?

_No response_
